visual_features.overall_style: Modern, clean, and confident with a polished SaaS-like aesthetic applied to a life-coaching context. Strong use of a dark teal brand background, large bold typography, and a single high-quality hero image. Minimalist chrome and generous spacing create a premium, calm but business-focused feel.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven primarily by size, contrast, and positioning. The hero heading is the dominant element: extremely large, bold, white text against a dark teal background on the left. The secondary focal point is the hero image on the right, occupying roughly half the hero width. Above the heading, a small green 'Blog' pill tag provides context but remains subtle. At the very top, logo + navigation form a clear global hierarchy: logo on the far left, inline navigation items in the center, then user/cart icons and a highly contrasted green CTA button ('Book a Session') on the far right. The eye is pulled from logo → nav → CTA, then down to the hero heading → hero image.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is generous and intentional. The hero section has large padding on all sides, especially top and left, which lets the heading breathe. The text block has ample line spacing and generous margins from the top nav and from the hero image. Navigation items are spaced broadly, avoiding a cramped look. Overall, horizontal and vertical gutters are wide, contributing to a spacious, high-end feel.
unique_elements: Large circular line-art shapes subtly overlaid in the background (bottom left and bottom right), adding visual interest without compromising readability.
Monoline circular logo mark that visually echoes the circular background motifs.
Pill-shaped label for 'Blog' that mirrors the pill-shaped primary CTA, reinforcing a cohesive rounded motif.
Consistent dark, immersive canvas (full-width dark teal hero and header) with content floating above it, giving a cinematic, focused hero.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implied 12-column responsive grid with a centered maximum content width (likely 1140–1280px). Hero content appears to span about 6–7 columns for text and 5–6 columns for the image.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width fixed header with horizontal navigation, followed by a full-width hero that uses a two-column split layout (text left, image right). Content is left-aligned within a centered content container.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top sticky/global header with logo, navigation links, icons, and a primary CTA. Directly beneath, a single, prominent hero section: contextual label, large title, and supporting image. Sections are clearly separated by substantial vertical spacing and by continuous background color rather than hard dividers.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Strong left alignment for all primary text elements and logo. Navigation items align horizontally along the header's vertical center line. The hero image aligns with the right edge of the central grid. Vertical alignment in the hero places the image aligned roughly top with the heading block, reinforcing balance between left and right columns.
responsive_hints: Two-column hero suggests that on smaller screens the layout will stack vertically with the heading above the image for a single-column flow.
Horizontal navigation with many links implies a breakpoint where it collapses into a hamburger menu or 'More' dropdown on tablet/phone.
The pill CTA likely becomes full-width or at least larger relative to the header elements on mobile for thumb usability.
Generous padding indicates that spacing can be reduced proportionally at smaller breakpoints without losing clarity.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow starting with a full-width header (logo left, nav centered-left, actions right) followed by a hero section organized in a two-column layout. Within the hero, content is arranged as: small category pill ('Blog') at top-left, then a large, multi-line heading block directly below it, occupying the left column. To the immediate right, a large rectangular photo fills the right column, aligned with the heading’s vertical mid/upper area. The text column is slightly wider than the image column, emphasizing the article title as the main content.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header consumes a relatively small vertical band at the top (roughly 10–15% of viewport height), while the hero occupies the majority of the above-the-fold area. Within the hero, roughly 55–60% width is allocated to the text column and 40–45% to the image column. Large outer margins and internal padding give the content breathing room, leaving noticeable empty dark teal space around the text block and between the image and the viewport edge.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Spacious editorial layout with low content density. Large fonts, generous line spacing, and significant padding around all elements reduce cognitive load. Only essential elements are shown above the fold: category, title, hero image, and global navigation. There is no clutter such as sidebars or secondary CTAs visible in this frame.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The visual flow primarily follows a horizontal F/Z-pattern: starting from the logo at the top-left, the eye moves across the navigation to the CTA on the top-right, then diagonally down to the 'Blog' pill and large heading on the left, and finally across to the hero image on the right. The strong contrast between the white heading and dark background anchors attention on the left before the imagery on the right is explored.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary CTA ('Book a Session') is a pill-shaped button with fully rounded ends (border-radius roughly 999px). It uses a bright, saturated green fill (#22C58E) against the dark teal header, with white or very dark teal text for strong legibility. The button has generous horizontal padding (approx. 20–24px per side) and moderate vertical padding, making it taller than navigation items. There is no visible heavy drop shadow; the contrast and color make it stand out, consistent with a flat or very lightly elevated style.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Implemented as a

ui_patterns.visual_description: While full card grids are not shown, the hero’s image treatment and the overall style suggest cards would be simple rectangles with subtle rounding (4–8px border-radius) and either a soft drop shadow or a faint border for separation. They would sit on a contrasting background (white card on light or dark teal background), with generous internal padding and clear typographic hierarchy (small category pill, title, short description). Shadows would be low-blur, low-opacity to maintain the minimal, modern aesthetic.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No form is visible in the screenshot, but given the design language the form fields would likely be full-width inputs with slightly rounded corners (4–6px radius), a 1px subtle border in a muted neutral or teal, and a light background (white or very light teal) on the dark sections. Labels would sit above inputs (stacked layout) with small, clean sans-serif text. Focus states likely use a stronger teal border or glow, matching the brand colors. Validation messages would be small text below inputs, in semantic colors (red for error). Overall form density would be moderate with comfortable vertical spacing between fields.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms would use a stacked layout: `<form><label>Field</label><input ... /></form>` with each label-input pair wrapped in a form-group container (`<div class="form-group">`). Grouping for multi-column layouts might use flexbox, but mobile-first they would be single-column.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Top navigation is a horizontal bar on a solid dark teal background. Logo on the far left (circular icon + 'Life Coach' wordmark), followed by evenly spaced text links ('About Me', 'Who I Help?', etc.) in a light color (white or near-white). Navigation items use a simple sans-serif typeface, likely medium weight. There is a subtle hover expectation (color change to a brighter green/teal or underline). Active state might be indicated via color change or a small underline. On the far right, minimalist line icons (user, cart) appear, followed by the green pill CTA.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Standard semantic structure: `

`. Icons likely implemented as inline SVGs or icon-font elements. The 'More' item indicates a dropdown pattern, which would be a nested
    inside that list item, displayed on hover/click.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The design suggests a preference for clean, card-like lists over dense text lists. Blog lists would likely be composed of spacious cards or rows with an image thumbnail on the left or top, followed by a bold title and short meta/excerpt. Separation between items would be achieved through generous spacing and/or subtle borders rather than heavy dividers. If simple text lists are used (e.g., in footers), they would be low-density with ample line-height and light text color on the dark background.

heading.visual_style: Bold geometric or neo-grotesque sans-serif with large x-height, even stroke widths, and strong presence. The hero title is heavy, clear, and modern, emphasizing clarity and strength over ornamentation.

body.visual_style: Clean, humanist sans-serif that remains highly readable at smaller sizes, with softer curves than the headings but still modern. Suitable for navigation, paragraphs, and labels.

spacing_system.component_padding: Common padding for interactive components (buttons, pills, tags) appears to be 8–12px vertically and 16–24px horizontally (2–3x the base unit). Larger blocks like hero text containers likely use 32–48px internal padding.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., between header and hero, or between hero and next section) is likely 64px or more, consistent with the spacious editorial feel.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely change color on hover (e.g., from white to a brighter teal/green) and may add a subtle underline or bottom border.
Buttons likely have hover states with slightly darker or lighter background shade, plus a possible increase in box-shadow or subtle scale-up (1.02) for tactile feedback.
The 'Blog' pill and similar tags may darken or show a faint shadow on hover, indicating clickability.
Hero image might have a gentle zoom-in or parallax effect on hover or scroll to enhance visual engagement without being distracting.
Dropdown under 'More' probably uses a fade and slide-down transition for smooth appearance.
Micro-interactions for icons (user, cart) might include color change and slight scale on hover, maintaining consistency with button interactions.
